title: Sloppy Joes
ingredients: 1 tbsp chopped pickled hot peppers (jalapenos or Peppadews)
1 ½ pounds ground beef, turkey or chicken
4-6 hamburger buns, lightly buttered and toasted under broiler
instructions: In a food processor, combine onion, peppers, garlic. Pulse until very finely minced.
Heat oil in large skillet, add the onion mixture and saute 4-5 minutes. Add the spices, stir well and saute another 4 minutes or until onions are very soft.
Add the ground meat and saute, breaking up clumps with a spoon until the meat is browned, about 5 minutes.
In a large measuring cup, whisk together the ketchup, tomato puree, and vinegar. Add this to the meat, bring to a simmer, and cook uncovered for 10 minutes or until slightly thickened.
Season with salt and pepper. Scoop sloppy joes onto each bun bottom, top, and serve. Good eaten with dill pickles.
